2605.22612.title: Healthcare LLM Benchmarks Are Only as Good as Their Explicit Assumptions
2605.22612.paper_summary: Benchmarks are necessary for healthcare evaluation, but are not sufficient for predicting deployment performance. Our position is that the evaluation--deployment gap arises not because of poorly designed benchmarks, but from implicit assumptions about how users interact with models that cannot be surfaced from benchmarks alone. To make this precise, we propose a classification of assumptions into two categories: task, which can be tested from conversation data alone, and outcome, which requires outcome data and behavioral studies for testing. Critically, outcome assumptions depend on human behavior, something that even well-designed benchmarks cannot directly observe. To demonstrate the operationality of this framework, we retrospectively analyze a healthcare RCT as a case study and find that the gap naturally separates into task and outcome gaps of roughly equal size. To address this, we make two contributions: first, we propose BenchmarkCards, an artifact that documents assumptions, and second, we propose staged evaluation, a procedure that systematically tests assumptions and evaluates performance.
2605.22612.paper_summary_zh: 基準對於醫療評估是必要的，但不足以預測部署性能。我們的立場是，評估與部署之間的差距並不是因為基準設計不良，而是來自於對用戶如何與模型互動的隱性假設，這些假設無法僅從基準中顯現出來。為了使這一點更為精確，我們將假設分類為兩個類別：任務，這可以僅從對話數據中進行測試；以及結果，這需要結果數據和行為研究來進行測試。關鍵是，結果假設依賴於人類行為，而即使是設計良好的基準也無法直接觀察到這一點。為了展示這一框架的可操作性，我們回顧性地分析了一個醫療隨機對照試驗作為案例研究，發現這一差距自然地分為大致相等大小的任務差距和結果差距。為了解決這一問題，我們提出了兩項貢獻：首先，我們提出了BenchmarkCards，一種記錄假設的工具；其次，我們提出了分階段評估，一種系統性測試假設和評估性能的程序。
2605.22612.author: Naveen Raman et.al.
2605.22612.authors: Naveen Raman, Santiago Cortes-Gomez, Mateo Dulce Rubio, Fei Fang, Bryan Wilder
